center.name: 勿里洞岛
center.summary: 勿里洞岛是图谱中的地点节点，当前关联5种鸟类，包括Bonaparte's nightjar、Sunda frogmouth、凤头鹰雕等5种鸟类，主要用于表示这些物种的分布地、停歇地或观测地。

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: It is native to the islands of Sumatra, Belitung and Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: |year=2024 |title=Pellorneum rostratum |volume=2024 |article-number=e.T22715792A260307448 |doi=10.2305/IUCN.UK.2024-2.RLTS.T22715792A260307448.en |access-date=27 May 2025}} | genus = Pellorneum | species = rostratum | authority = (Blyth, 1842) | synonyms = }} The Malayan swamp babbler (Pellorneum rostratum), previously named the white-chested babbler, is a species of bird in the ground babbler family, Pellorneidae, that is found in the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, the Riau Islands, the Lingga Islands and the island of Belitung

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: This species is a native resident of lowland forests across southern Thailand, southern Myanmar, the Malay Peninsula, Sumatra, Riau Archipelago, and the islands of Banjak, Bangka, Belitung, Borneo, and Natuna. It hangs around the middle storeys of lowland semi-evergreen and evergreen forests, which include freshwater swamp forests, in lowlands and in hills. It is sometimes in the lower storeys and borders of forests, and in mature secondary growth forests but tends not to live in mangroves, in cultivated areas, or in open country. The presence of lianas is also favored by this species. The highest sightings of individuals vary depending on the location: in the Malay Peninsula, the highest sighting was at , and on Mount Kinabalu, it was at , while it was only about in Thailand. In Kalimantan, the height the birds were inhabiting varied between

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: cornutus is found in Sumatra, the Indonesian islands Bangka and Belitung, as well as Banggi island and Borneo

birds.label: 分布于
birds.evidence: In Indonesia, it occurs in Sumatra, Simeulue and Mentawai off the west side and Riau, Bangka Island and Belitung, Java, Borneo; and the western and southern Philippines on Palawan, Calamian Islands, Lubang Island, Mindoro, Mindanao and Bohol. The changeable hawk-eagle inhabits savannawoodland, agricultural land, wetlands, tea plantations, forest villages and even suburbs
